_meta.purpose: Hand-curated per-candidate rationales rendered into executive_summary.pdf.
_meta.rule: Each entry must explain the case to an intelligent non-specialist. Required fields: one_liner (MUST end with '→ Potential outcomes: ...' listing the plausible resolutions), hypothesis (one sentence — the bet), thesis (why we believe it, with plain-English context), expected_outcome (what happens if we're right), price_targets (dict: reference_price, upside_base, upside_best, downside), time_sensitivity (urgency label + concrete window), kill_watch (what would invalidate), catalyst_date_iso.

TVTX.one_liner: FDA decision on FILSPARI for rare kidney disease (FSGS); PDUFA April 13 already passed. → Potential outcomes: FDA approval (+20-35%), short delay then approval, 3-month major amendment (flat), or Complete Response Letter (-40-60%).
TVTX.hypothesis: The FDA grants traditional approval of FILSPARI (sparsentan) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS) on or shortly after April 13, 2026, making TVTX the only company with an approved drug for this disease.
TVTX.thesis: FSGS is a rare kidney disease that scars the kidney's filter and causes kidney failure — there is currently NO approved treatment. FILSPARI is already approved in IgA Nephropathy (a related kidney disease) and has shown benefit in FSGS in Phase 3 trials. The FDA accepted the supplemental application (sNDA) under Priority Review. Note: the PDUFA date has already passed (April 13) — TVTX is now in a waiting/delay state. The market often over-discounts drugs stuck in post-PDUFA limbo; if the delay is short (amendment-style), the stock re-rates when approval lands. If the delay becomes a Complete Response Letter (CRL), the stock drops hard.
TVTX.expected_outcome: If approval lands within 2-4 weeks of PDUFA (minor delay): stock bumps +20-35% as the first and only FSGS drug. If CRL: stock drops 40-60%. Base case (a 3-month major amendment pushing PDUFA to July): stock drifts sideways.
TVTX.time_sensitivity: MEDIUM — PDUFA April 13 already passed without approval (warning sign). Most info emerges next 30-60 days. Entry window narrower than pre-PDUFA.
TVTX.kill_watch: FDA issues CRL. FDA 3-month major amendment extension (trim). Chinook/Novartis positive Phase 3 in FSGS.

RPAY.one_liner: Activist Forager crossed 12.9% of Repay right as the board adopted a poison pill with a 12.5% trigger — confrontation imminent. → Potential outcomes: acquisition by Forager or a strategic buyer, negotiated board seats + strategic review, public proxy fight, or Forager retreats.
RPAY.hypothesis: Forager Fund's next 13D/A or public campaign (within 2-4 weeks) forces RPAY to negotiate (board seats, strategic review, sale) or fight a proxy contest — either drives the stock meaningfully higher from $3.18.
RPAY.thesis: Activist investing = a fund buys a large stake and pressures management for changes (board seats, sale, spinoffs). Poison pill = a defense: if any investor exceeds a threshold (12.5%), others get cheap shares that dilute the activist. RPAY adopted this pill on 2026-04-13 with an unusually tight 12.5% trigger. The same day, Forager Fund (Forager Capital Management, Birmingham AL; MacArthur/Kissel) filed a 13D/A disclosing 12.9% — ABOVE the trigger. Federal rules: Forager has 10 business days after material position changes to file. Next move (expected end-April / early-May 2026) will announce intent: board nominees, tender offer, or settlement demand. RPAY is a small-cap payments processor near multi-year lows — classic activist target. Pedro missed this name earlier because time sensitivity was unclear; entry window is hours-to-weeks, not months.
RPAY.expected_outcome: Most likely (60%): negotiated settlement — Forager gets 1-2 board seats / strategic review, stock +30-80% to $4.00-5.50. Less likely (20%): Forager forces sale at $5-7 (+55% to +120%). Fight scenario (15%): proxy drags, but announcement alone adds +15-25%. Bad case (5%): activist retreats, stock back to $2.80.

price_targets.upside_base: $4.00-5.50 (+26% to +73%) — negotiated settlement with board seats

RPAY.time_sensitivity: VERY HIGH — Forager's next 13D/A could file any business day from now through early May 2026 (10-business-day rule). Tight 12.5% poison pill trigger invites litigation. Entry window NOW through next 2-4 weeks — once campaign goes public the first 15-25% is already priced. THIS IS THE KIND OF SITUATION PEDRO ASKED NOT TO MISS AGAIN.
RPAY.kill_watch: Forager 13D/A reducing stake below 12.5% (kills thesis). RPAY board announces strategic review BEFORE Forager's next move (cheap — most upside priced in). SEC investigates Forager disclosure timing. RPAY earnings miss collapses the business-quality premise.

VRDN.one_liner: Viridian's stock crashed 51% on issues unrelated to the drug being reviewed on June 30 (Thyroid Eye Disease). → Potential outcomes: FDA approval with broad label (+36-105%), approval with narrow label (+20-40%), or Complete Response Letter (-45-66%).
VRDN.hypothesis: The FDA approves veligrotug for Thyroid Eye Disease on June 30, 2026, and the stock re-rates from $14.66 toward $20-30 (+36% to +105%) as the dislocation-driven selloff reverses.
VRDN.thesis: Thyroid Eye Disease (TED) is a disfiguring autoimmune condition where eye muscles swell and bulge. Only one drug is approved (Horizon/Amgen Tepezza, 8 infusions). Viridian's veligrotug has THREE advantages: (a) only 5 infusions vs. 8 — fewer visits, better compliance; (b) two positive Phase 3 trials covering BOTH active AND chronic TED (Tepezza is used mostly in active); (c) better hearing-loss safety (5.5% placebo-adjusted vs. ~10% — hearing loss is the most feared side effect). The BLA has Priority Review AND Breakthrough Therapy. WHY the crash from $34 to $14.66: (1) Viridian's DIFFERENT drug — elegrobart — reported Phase 3 data March 30 that disappointed investors, 27% one-day drop; (2) Amgen's positive Phase 3 for subcutaneous Tepezza in early April raised competitive fears. CRITICAL: neither affects veligrotug's approval probability. Stock now below even the most bearish analyst target ($20).
VRDN.expected_outcome: Approval June 30 (high probability given data package): stock rebounds toward low-end sellside targets. Best case (broad label covering active + chronic, clean safety): mid-$20s. Ceiling $30-34 only if elegrobart REVEAL-2 also positive. Worst case (CRL): $5-8.
VRDN.time_sensitivity: HIGH — PDUFA June 30, 2026 (74 days). Enter now — 10+ weeks of runway allows accumulation without paying event-week premium. Elegrobart REVEAL-2 readout in Q2 provides secondary catalyst.
VRDN.kill_watch: FDA information request signaling 'Major Amendment' (pushes PDUFA 3 months). Hearing-loss safety signal in post-market Tepezza updates. Elegrobart REVEAL-2 misses badly + management signals dilution.

AXSM.one_liner: FDA decision April 30 for the first-ever drug approved specifically for Alzheimer's agitation — a $5B+ unmet need. → Potential outcomes: clean approval with broad label (+15-30%, sustained re-rating), narrow label approval (+5-10%), or Complete Response Letter (-21-29%).
AXSM.hypothesis: The FDA approves AXS-05 (dextromethorphan/bupropion) for Alzheimer's Disease Agitation on April 30, 2026, and AXSM re-rates as the market recognizes a new multi-billion dollar franchise into an unmet need.
AXSM.thesis: Alzheimer's Disease Agitation (ADA) is one of the most distressing symptoms of Alzheimer's — aggression, restlessness, shouting — and currently NO FDA-approved drug exists for it (physicians use antipsychotics off-label, which carry black-box warnings in the elderly). AXS-05 is already approved for major depression (as Auvelity) and now seeks the ADA indication. Phase 3 ACCORD met its primary endpoint; the label, if granted, unlocks access to 30%+ of Alzheimer's patients. AXSM is a mid-cap specialty pharma with commercial infrastructure in place from Auvelity. Even modest ADA penetration translates to $1-2B in annual sales within 3-4 years. The market has partially priced approval but is leaving most launch value on the table because biotech investors discount pre-launch catalysts until proof-of-commercial-traction arrives.
AXSM.expected_outcome: Approval + clean label (no black-box, broad population): +15-30% on the day, sustained re-rating. Approval narrow label: +5-10%. CRL: -20 to -35%.

price_targets.upside_best: $260-300 (+41% to +63%) — broad label + analyst model upgrades post-launch

AXSM.time_sensitivity: HIGH — PDUFA April 30 (13 days). Position should already be built or completed in next 3-5 trading days. After day 10 before PDUFA, IV premium prohibitive for new entries.
AXSM.kill_watch: FDA Major Amendment delay (PDUFA pushed 3 months). Negative AdCom surprise (note: no AdCom currently scheduled, mildly positive). New safety signal in post-market Auvelity.

VERA.one_liner: Small-cap biotech with the best Phase 3 data in IgA Nephropathy, heading into FDA decision July 7. → Potential outcomes: approval + broadest label (+78-113%), approval + competitive label (+37-61%), or Complete Response Letter (-48-57%).
VERA.hypothesis: The FDA approves atacicept for IgA Nephropathy on July 7, 2026, and the stock re-rates 40-60% as the market recognizes atacicept is not a me-too entry — it has the strongest efficacy in class.
VERA.thesis: IgA Nephropathy (IgAN) is an autoimmune kidney disease where mis-folded antibodies clog the kidney filter. The market already has approved drugs (Travere's FILSPARI, Calliditas's TARPEYO), but atacicept targets the upstream B-cell driver via BAFF/APRIL inhibition. Phase 3 (ORIGIN-3) showed a 46% proteinuria reduction at 36 weeks — the best in class. Priority Review granted, NO AdCom required — both positive. WHY the stock is beaten down: small-cap biotechs with a single product and pending PDUFA routinely suffer 'approval anxiety' dislocations — investors rotate out into the event and back in on approval. The current price implies high-30s-percent probability of approval; the actual probability based on the data package is 75-85%.
VERA.expected_outcome: Approval + clean label: +50-80% over the following month as analysts update models (atacicept would leapfrog the class). Approval narrow label (stage-limited): +25-40%. CRL: -50 to -65%.

price_targets.upside_best: $75-90 (+78% to +113%) — approval with broadest label + acquisition interest

VERA.time_sensitivity: HIGH — PDUFA July 7, 2026 (81 days). Enter now or over next 2-3 weeks — after mid-May the event-week premium compresses entry economics. No AdCom scheduled (positive); watch for last-minute AdCom announcement.
VERA.kill_watch: FDA announces AdCom (implies regulatory uncertainty). Major Amendment extension pushes PDUFA to October. Novartis iptacopan or Chinook atrasentan report unexpectedly strong Phase 3.

AVNS.one_liner: Avanos merger arbitrage: going private at $25 cash vs. $24.66 — a 1.4% spread closable by late 2026. → Potential outcomes: deal closes at $25 (+1.4%), superior strategic bid emerges (+7-9%), or deal breaks (-19-27%, low probability).
AVNS.hypothesis: The American Industrial Partners (AIP) all-cash acquisition of AVNS closes on schedule in Q3/Q4 2026, delivering $25.00 per share (vs. current $24.66) — annualized 3-5% spread return with low deal-break risk.
AVNS.thesis: Merger arbitrage = you buy the stock of a company that has agreed to be acquired and capture the remaining spread between market price and deal price. The spread compensates for (a) deal-break risk, (b) time value of money, (c) antitrust or financing risk. AVNS announced on 2026-04-14 a definitive agreement to be acquired by American Industrial Partners (AIP), a large PE firm, at $25.00 cash per share. Medical devices is a consolidating, well-understood PE sector; AIP has financing committed; no obvious antitrust concerns (AVNS is a niche mid-cap in respiratory + wound-care). The 1.4% gross spread is modest but the deal-break probability is low (<8%).
AVNS.expected_outcome: Deal closes Q3/Q4 2026 at $25.00 — gross return 1.4%, annualized 3-6% depending on close date. Low vol, stable spread.
AVNS.time_sensitivity: LOW — Definitive agreement signed; expected close Q3/Q4 2026 (6-9 months). Hold-to-close trade, not timing trade. Good for capital preservation / cash-plus return; NOT where outsized gains live.
AVNS.kill_watch: Any material adverse change (MAC) disclosed in proxy. HSR antitrust second request (delays 3-6 months). AIP financing backup failure. A superior strategic proposal — upside, not kill.

GSAT.one_liner: Amazon acquiring Globalstar — mixed cash + stock (0.3x AMZN) deal with 2-3% spread + embedded AMZN call option. → Potential outcomes: deal closes with AMZN flat (+2-5%), deal closes + AMZN rally (+10-15%), or FCC/DOJ blocks on antitrust grounds (-19-31%).
GSAT.hypothesis: Amazon's acquisition of Globalstar at $90 cash + 0.3x AMZN share per GSAT share closes by late 2026 / early 2027 with minimal regulatory friction.
GSAT.thesis: Globalstar operates a satellite communications network — the company behind Apple's iPhone emergency satellite connectivity. Amazon announced on 2026-04-14 a definitive agreement to acquire GSAT with mixed consideration: $90 cash plus 0.3 AMZN per GSAT share. At $80.03 current, the paper spread depends on AMZN (fluctuates). Amazon is building out Project Kuiper; Globalstar's spectrum is a strategic fit. Antitrust review is the main risk — not market concentration (telecom regulatory terrain is messy) but Amazon being under increased government scrutiny. Structural caveat: because part of the consideration is AMZN stock, you are buying both the arb AND implicit long AMZN — hedge accordingly.
GSAT.expected_outcome: Deal closes Q4 2026 - Q1 2027: base-case return 2-4% gross. Material AMZN rally would boost total economics; AMZN selloff reduces them.

price_targets.upside_best: $88-92 (+10% to +15%) — deal closes AND AMZN rallies 15%+

GSAT.time_sensitivity: LOW — Definitive agreement signed; expected close Q4 2026 / Q1 2027 (8-12 months). Entry window open; no urgency.
GSAT.kill_watch: FCC or DOJ signals serious spectrum-transfer concerns. Amazon becomes target of a breakup / antitrust freeze. AMZN sells off sharply — stock-consideration hurts arb economics.

SEM.one_liner: Select Medical take-private by WCAS + Ortenzio family at ~$16.50 cash — low-risk LBO with ~3% spread. → Potential outcomes: deal closes as announced (+0.7%), superior strategic/PE proposal emerges (+7-10%), or state hospital regulators block the transfer (-21-33%).
SEM.hypothesis: WCAS + Ortenzio family take-private of SEM at ~$16.50 cash per share closes by Q4 2026, delivering a ~3% spread with low break probability given insider-involvement structure.
SEM.thesis: Take-private = a public company is acquired by private investors and delisted. WCAS (Welsh Carson Anderson & Stowe) is a large PE firm specializing in healthcare; the Ortenzio family founded Select Medical and already own a large stake. When founder families partner with PE to take a company private, the deal-break probability is very low (family is committed, PE has long-term capital). Select Medical operates specialty hospitals and rehab facilities — a well-understood, cash-flowing business. The ~3% spread reflects high probability of close. Main regulatory risk: state-level hospital ownership transfer approvals (minor friction).
SEM.expected_outcome: Deal closes Q4 2026 at ~$16.50: gross return ~3%, annualized 5-7% depending on close date. Low vol, stable spread, capital-preservation sleeve.

price_targets.upside_best: $17.50-18.00 (+7% to +10%) — superior proposal from strategic / larger PE

SEM.time_sensitivity: LOW — Definitive agreement signed; expected close Q4 2026 (6-8 months). Hold-to-close trade.
SEM.kill_watch: State hospital regulators block transfer in any material state. WCAS financing falls through. Material operational miss at SEM before close triggers MAC clause.

RGR.one_liner: Beretta Holding has filed a proxy fight against Sturm Ruger — the Italian firearms giant is taking on the American icon. → Potential outcomes: negotiated settlement with board seats (+10-20%), strategic transaction / takeover (+25-40%), or Beretta loses the vote but stays as holder (-5-9%).
RGR.hypothesis: Beretta Holding's proxy contest at Sturm Ruger (RGR) resolves in a negotiated settlement or a successful slate within the May-June 2026 AM window, driving the stock +10-30% from $41.98.
RGR.thesis: A proxy fight = a shareholder nominates their own board candidates seeking to replace incumbents. Beretta Holding — the family-controlled Italian conglomerate that owns Beretta firearms, Benelli, and Sako — accumulated a ~10% stake in Sturm Ruger (RGR) and filed proxy materials to nominate their own slate. Ruger is the iconic American firearms maker. The filing signals either (a) Beretta wants consolidation in the global firearms industry, (b) Beretta sees RGR as strategically undervalued and wants operational changes, or (c) Beretta is positioning for a larger stake or acquisition. Any of these resolutions tends to be stock-positive: Beretta wins and pushes changes; RGR settles with board seats; or a strategic transaction is announced.
RGR.expected_outcome: Most likely: Beretta + Ruger negotiate a settlement before the AM — Beretta gets 2-3 seats and governance concessions, stock +10-20%. Less likely but higher value: strategic transaction announced (+25-40%). Downside bounded: even if Beretta loses the vote, the 10% stake is a floor.

price_targets.upside_base: $46-50 (+10% to +20%) — negotiated settlement with board seats
price_targets.downside: $38-40 (-5% to -9%) — Beretta loses vote but stays as holder

RGR.time_sensitivity: MEDIUM-HIGH — AM date not yet fixed (placeholder in filings). Historically late April to early May; contest filing pattern suggests late May or June 2026. Ruger's DEF 14A (company's definitive proxy) will set the date — expected within 2-4 weeks. Enter now while volume is still low; window narrows once the date is announced.
RGR.kill_watch: Beretta withdraws proxy materials without a settlement. ISS + Glass Lewis both recommend AGAINST Beretta's slate. Ruger adopts poison pill with exclusionary trigger. Firearms-sector liability verdict shocks RGR fundamentals.
